goal: Render the UR10e+EOAT swept workspace envelope and return XYZ extents in mm for a 200mm-offset light-curtain guard.
thoughts: Swept volume (reachable set expanded by EOAT geometry) differs from pure IK-reachable point cloud -- confirm which show_workspace produces. EOAT must be included; add its 180mm extension on top. Give extents mm-precise so she can site the curtain. Top-down capture helps eyeball against her floor plan.
code:
ws = show_workspace(
    articulation_path="/World/UR10e",
    include_end_effector=True,
    mode="swept_volume",
    resolution_mm=10
)
# or:
visualize_clearance(
    subject="/World/UR10e",
    others=["/World/Cell/Guard"],
    envelope_offset_mm=200
)
bbox = get_bounding_box(prim_path=ws.prim_path, units="mm")
extents_mm = {
    "x_min": bbox.min[0], "x_max": bbox.max[0],
    "y_min": bbox.min[1], "y_max": bbox.max[1],
    "z_min": bbox.min[2], "z_max": bbox.max[2]
}
capture_viewport(
    path="<TOPDOWN_PNG>",
    camera_path="/World/Cameras/TopDown",
    width=2048, height=2048
)
failure_modes: Confusing reachable point cloud with swept volume (differs by EOAT extent)
Forgetting to include EOAT so the guard sits 180mm inside the real envelope
Claiming STEP export of the envelope works without checking supported export formats
